user.title: Profile Setup
user.description: Create a new agent profile with a unique name and personality.

data_description.profile_name: A friendly name for this conversation profile.
data_description.server_type: Choose which provider or inference server this profile uses.

server.title: Server Configuration
server.description: Configure your inference server URL and MCP server port.

data_description.lmstudio_url: The base URL for the local or OpenAI-compatible server used by this profile.
data_description.api_key: The API key or bearer token required by this provider.
data_description.mcp_port: The local port used by the shared MCP server.
data_description.openclaw_host: Host name or IP address of the OpenClaw gateway.
data_description.openclaw_port: Port used by the OpenClaw gateway WebSocket server.
data_description.openclaw_token: Bearer token used to authenticate with the OpenClaw gateway.
data_description.openclaw_use_ssl: Use a secure WebSocket connection when talking to the OpenClaw gateway.

openclaw_pairing.title: OpenClaw Device Pairing

data_description.model_name: The LLM model this profile should use.
data_description.system_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective system prompt. Edit it to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.
data_description.technical_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective technical instructions. Edit them to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.

model_fields.name: Model
model_fields.description: Choose the model this profile should use.

data_description.model_name: The LLM model this profile should use.

prompts.name: Prompts
prompts.description: Review the current effective prompts, copy them, or edit them for this profile.

data_description.system_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective system prompt. Edit it to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.
data_description.technical_prompt: Prefilled with the current effective technical instructions. Edit them to customize this profile. If left unchanged, the integration keeps using the built-in prompt from code.

conversation.name: Conversation
conversation.description: Behavior, follow-up handling, and voice-output settings for this profile.

data_description.control_home_assistant: Allow this profile to execute Home Assistant actions instead of only answering questions.
data_description.response_mode: Controls whether the assistant ends immediately or asks natural follow-up questions when appropriate.
data_description.follow_up_phrases: Comma-separated phrases used to detect follow-up style responses.
data_description.end_words: Comma-separated words or phrases that should end the conversation.
data_description.clean_responses: Trim unnecessary formatting or filler to keep spoken responses cleaner.

performance.name: Performance
performance.description: Model-response limits, execution limits, timeout, and debugging.

data_description.temperature: Controls response randomness. Lower values are more deterministic.
data_description.max_tokens: Caps how many tokens the model may generate in one response.
data_description.max_history: How many prior conversation messages to keep in context.
data_description.max_iterations: Caps how many tool-calling turns the assistant may use for one request.
data_description.timeout: How long to wait for a provider response before timing out.
data_description.debug_mode: Enable more detailed logging for troubleshooting.

provider.name: Provider
provider.description: Provider-specific settings shown only when they apply.

data_description.ollama_keep_alive: How long Ollama should keep the selected model loaded in memory.
data_description.ollama_num_ctx: Override the context window size passed to Ollama. Use 0 to keep the model default.
data_description.openclaw_session_key: Name of the OpenClaw session to send messages to.

tools.name: Tools
tools.description: Unchecked tools remain available whenever they are enabled on the shared MCP server. Check a box to forbid a tool family for just this profile.

data_description.disable_assist_bridge: Prevents this profile from using Home Assistant's native Assist tools, prompt, and context snapshot.
data_description.disable_calculator: Prevents this profile from using math and expression-evaluation tools.
data_description.disable_custom_tools: Prevents this profile from using external custom tool packages loaded through the shared MCP server.
data_description.disable_device: Prevents this profile from discovering physical devices and device details.
data_description.disable_memory: Prevents this profile from storing, recalling, or forgetting shared persisted memories.
data_description.disable_music_assistant: Prevents this profile from using Music Assistant search, queue, library, and playback tools.
data_description.disable_read_url: Prevents this profile from opening and extracting page content from URLs.
data_description.disable_recorder: Prevents this profile from using recorder-backed history, count, duration, and point-in-time tools.
data_description.disable_response_service: Prevents this profile from using Home Assistant services that return structured data, such as calendar and forecast reads.
data_description.disable_search: Prevents this profile from using the built-in web search helper.
data_description.disable_unit_conversion: Prevents this profile from using exact unit-conversion tools such as °C to °F, distance, storage, and data-rate conversions.
data_description.disable_weather_forecast: Prevents this profile from using the dedicated Home Assistant weather forecast helper.
data_description.disable_web_search: Prevents this profile from using web search and URL-reading tools, even if they are enabled on the shared MCP server.

mcp_server.title: Shared MCP Server Settings
mcp_server.description: ⚠️ These settings affect ALL profiles. Changes here will impact every MCP Assist conversation agent.

data_description.mcp_port: The local port used by the shared MCP server.
data_description.allowed_ips: Optional comma-separated IP addresses or CIDR ranges that may connect in addition to localhost.

context.name: Context
context.description: Control which Home Assistant identity and location details are included in AI prompts and MCP tool-call payloads.

data.include_current_user_in_tool_calls: Share Current User with MCP Tools
data.include_home_location_in_tool_calls: Share Home Location with MCP Tools

data_description.include_current_user: Include the current Home Assistant user's name in the AI prompt when available.
data_description.include_home_location: Include a compact home-location summary in the AI prompt, using Home Assistant's configured home name and coordinates.
data_description.include_current_user_in_tool_calls: Send the current Home Assistant user ID and name in MCP tools/call context so tool packages can audit or personalize actions.
data_description.include_home_location_in_tool_calls: Send Home Assistant's configured home name, latitude, and longitude in MCP tools/call context for tool packages that need location metadata.

discovery.name: Discovery
discovery.description: Control how the shared MCP server builds and returns discovery results.

data_description.enable_gap_filling: Builds a compact system index to keep discovery fast and context-aware.
data_description.max_entities_per_discovery: Caps how many entities a single discovery call may return.

memory.name: Memory
memory.description: Configure how long shared persisted memories are kept when the Memory tool is enabled.

data_description.memory_default_ttl_days: Default number of days a new memory is kept when the tool call does not provide ttl_days.
data_description.memory_max_ttl_days: Maximum number of days any stored memory may be retained.
data_description.memory_max_items: Upper bound on active stored memories. Oldest memories are pruned first when this limit is exceeded.

tools.name: Tools
tools.description: Enable or disable optional tool capabilities exposed by the shared MCP server.

data_description.enable_assist_bridge: Expose Home Assistant's native Assist tools, prompt, and context snapshot.
data_description.enable_external_custom_tools: Load additional local tool packages from the Home Assistant config directory under mcp-assist-tools. Disabled by default. Only enable trusted packages because they run as Python code inside Home Assistant and may add MCP tools plus short prompt guidance.
data_description.enable_device_tools: Expose physical-device discovery and device detail tools.
data_description.enable_memory_tools: Expose shared persisted memory tools so the assistant can remember, recall, and forget user-approved facts and preferences with TTL-based expiry.
data_description.enable_music_assistant_support: Expose Music Assistant search, queue, library, and playback tools.
data_description.enable_read_url_tool: Expose URL reading so the assistant can inspect specific web pages.
data_description.enable_recorder_tools: Expose recorder-backed history, analysis, and point-in-time tools.
data_description.enable_response_service_tools: Expose Home Assistant services that return structured data, such as calendar and forecast reads.
data_description.enable_search_tool: Expose web search using the configured shared provider.
data_description.enable_unit_conversion_tools: Expose exact unit-conversion tools such as °C to °F, distance, storage, and data-rate conversions.
data_description.enable_weather_forecast_tool: Expose the dedicated Home Assistant weather forecast helper.
data_description.enable_web_search: Expose web search and URL-reading tools.
data_description.search_provider: Choose which provider to use when Web Search is enabled.
data_description.brave_api_key: Required only when Web Search is enabled and Brave Search is selected.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the inference server. Make sure it's running and accessible.
error.no_models: No models loaded on the server. Please load a model first.
error.invalid_model: The specified model is not available on the server.
error.invalid_ip: Invalid IP address or CIDR range format. Use comma-separated values like: 172.30.0.0/16, 192.168.1.100
error.invalid_json: Invalid JSON format. Please check your MCP servers configuration.
error.invalid_api_key: Invalid API key. Please check and try again.
error.auth_failed: Authentication failed. Please verify your API key.
error.openclaw_not_paired: Device not yet approved on the OpenClaw server. Please approve it and try again.
error.openclaw_connection_failed: Could not connect to the OpenClaw gateway. Check the host, port, and token.

abort.already_configured: A profile with this name already exists. Please choose a different name.
